body:
## Context

`.github/workflows/preview.yml` runs as a single `pull_request` workflow with three jobs — `build` (builds the site, no token), `deploy` (holds `id-token`, calls the preview API at `preview-api.sepo.sh`), and `teardown`. Because `pull_request` runs the workflow **from the PR's own head branch**, the privileged `deploy` job is defined by the pull request itself, so a PR could modify the job that holds the OIDC token.

## Goal

Move the privileged deploy to **trusted default-branch code** via the standard `workflow_run` pattern, so a PR cannot alter what runs with the token. Split the single workflow into two:

- **`preview-build.yml`** — `on: pull_request`. Builds the site (`eval "$AGENT_PREVIEW_BUILD_COMMAND"`) and uploads it as an artifact. No privileged permissions (it holds no token, so running the PR's version is fine).
- **`preview-deploy.yml`** — `on: workflow_run` (fires when the build workflow completes). Runs the **default branch's** version. Holds `id-token: write` (+ `pull-requests: write` for the comment). Mints the OIDC token, calls the preview API with the build artifact, posts the sticky preview comment, and handles teardown on PR close. Runs **no PR-controlled code** (no checkout, no build).

The deploy workflow identifies the PR from the triggering build run's metadata (head branch / PR number) and passes the build artifact to the preview API.

## Acceptance

- Agent PRs (and `sepo-preview`-labeled PRs) still auto-preview; `no-preview` still opts out; `AGENT_PREVIEW_POLICY` behavior unchanged.
- The privileged deploy job runs only trusted default-branch code — no PR build or checkout.
- Teardown on PR close still removes the preview.
- No secrets are added to the repo (deploy authenticates via GitHub Actions OIDC).

## Dependency

This relies on the preview service deriving the preview identity from the build artifact's provenance (rather than from the PR's OIDC token), because a `workflow_run` deploy's token attests the default branch, not the PR. That service-side change is coordinated separately — land and verify it before testing this end to end.

This supersedes the current single-workflow `preview.yml` (its three jobs move into the two split workflows).

title: Harden preview deploys: run the privileged step from the default branch (workflow_run)
